titleStmt.title: Letter from Leonard Webb to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, 10 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Leonard Webb

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Leonard Webb (1879-1953) to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington(1877-1946). Webb writes that he has enclosed a sum owing to Skeffington on the behalf of Miss Deborah Webb for a subscription. Enclosed in the letter are multiple receipts.
Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, suffragette, nationalist, language teacher, was the widow of Francis Sheehy-Skeffington (who was summarily executed on 26 April 1916). She was active during the Rising, bringing food to the Volunteers in the G.P.O. and the College of Surgeons. Four days passed before she found out what had happened to her husband, Francis (1878-1916), and it wasn't until almost two weeks later that the full details of his execution emerged.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; MS 33,604/16-34

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TELEPHONE No1678. 1 Suffolk Street
Dublin 10th of February, 1916.
Mrs Sheehy Skeffington, 11 Grosvenor Place,
Rathgar. Miss Deborah Webb. Dear Madam, I am requested by Miss Deborah Webb of 9 Garville Road,
Rathgar, to send you a cheque for £1.5.0. in respect of
a subscription which Miss Webb states she owes you. I have
pleasure in enclosing my cheque for £1.5.0. accordingly,
and shall be much obliged if you will acknowledge receipt
in course. Miss Webb states that you will know what the
subscription is for. Yours faithfully, Leonard Webb
267 OGLAIG NA HEIREANN
(IRISH VOLUNTEERS.)
CISTE COSNAM NA HEIREANN
(Defence of Ireland Fund.) 2 Dawson Street,
Dublin 25 . 1 . 1916 2 SRAIO OSRUIN,
OAILE AT-CLIAT. FUAIREAO O "Irish Citizen" Concert
Received from AN MEIO REO O' AIRGEAO Two shillings & sixpence
the sum of
(One inch Ad) 1 GCOIR CIRTE CORNAM NA HEIREANN
(for the Defence of Ireland Fund)
AGUR TAIMIO AN-OUIOEAC dhe MAR GEALL AIR.
for which we are much obliged. £2/6
BULMER HOBSON
MANAGER
Pooling
INVOICE. 13 Fleet Street,
Dublin , Febr 24th 1916 Irish Citizen Concert Committee
To NEW IRELAND PUBLISHING CO. Ltd., Dr.
PRINTERS AND PUBLISHERS.
No claims admitted or allowances made unless
received within 14 days of above date. For Advertisement 30
30 Paid with thanks P.J. Ritter
23 Feby 1916 Mrs Sheehy Skeffington 11 Grosvenor Place To Printing 25 Posters for Concert
2000 Hand Bills
500 Programmes
Allec 60
60
146
1166
16
£1150
PILLAR STUDIOS:
1 Nth. Earl St. & 34 Lr. Sackville St.
(OPPOSITE NELSON'S PILLAR) Irish Suffragist 19/2/16 Westmoreland St. To Hire Of Van Boy
(1Day)
Paid RW Moss 4/6
